name: mruby-lsp
description: Language server for mruby projects — completion, hover, and go-to-definition driven by the live mruby VM.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: mruby-lsp installs its runtime gems and runs your project's mruby build to power the server, so the build/install/server-start only run once you trust the workspace.

commands.title: mruby-lsp: Build/Setup Server For This Project
mruby-lsp: Update mruby (git pull the checkout)
mruby-lsp: Update Pulled-in Gems (refresh to latest)

mrubyLsp.rebuildOnSave.description: Rebuild the mruby VM and re-index when you save a build-relevant file. Per-workspace; runs your project's build, so only enable it for projects you trust.

mrubyLsp.requestTimeout.markdownDescription: Maximum seconds a single language request (hover, go-to-definition, completion, …) may run before the server abandons it and returns no result. Guards against a reflection or `addr2line` call wedging on pathological input so the editor never waits forever. Set to `0` to disable the cap (requests run unbounded).

mrubyLsp.mrdbPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `mrdb` debugger binary used by the mruby debug adapter. `mrdb` is built from your mruby checkout (the `mruby-bin-debugger` gem) — point this at e.g. `<mruby>/build/host/bin/mrdb`. Leave empty to auto-detect from your mruby build, then `PATH`.

mrubyLsp.debugTrace.description: Echo the full mrdb command/response dialog to the Debug Console while debugging (for diagnosing breakpoint binding or prompt parsing). Same as a launch config's "trace": true, but no launch.json needed — a launch config's own "trace" still wins.

mrubyLsp.debugStopOnEntry.description: Pause on the program's first executable line when launching the debugger (no launch.json needed). mrdb starts paused there anyway; this surfaces it so you can step/inspect from the start. A launch config's own "stopOnEntry" still wins.

mrubyLsp.rubyPath.description: Path to the ruby executable used to set up the bundled language server. Leave empty to auto-detect.

mrubyLsp.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the mruby language server in the output tab. Off by default; set to 'verbose' to log every request/response/notification with params and timing (also enables the server's per-request lifecycle logging).

program.description: Absolute path to the .rb (source) or .mrb (bytecode) file mrdb runs. mruby has no fixed entry point — this file IS the entry: it does whatever starts your program (top-level code, a main/__main__ call, or invoking a method defined in another gem). No arguments are passed to mrdb.

sourceDir.description: For a .mrb program: directory of the .rb sources it references, passed to mrdb as -d so listing and breakpoints can resolve. Defaults to the program's directory.

mrdb.description: Path to the mrdb binary. Defaults to the mrubyLsp.mrdbPath setting, else `mrdb` on PATH.

cwd.description: Working directory for the debugged program. Defaults to the program's directory.

stopOnEntry.description: Pause on the program's first line at launch. On by default (mrdb launches paused there anyway; this surfaces it so you can step/inspect from the start). Set false to run straight to your first breakpoint.

trace.description: Echo the full mrdb command/response dialog to the Debug Console (for diagnosing breakpoint binding or prompt parsing).

configurationSnippets.label: mruby: launch a .rb file under mrdb
configurationSnippets.description: Run and debug an mruby script with mrdb
